exchanges.question: May I know your name?
exchanges.example_answer: Yes, you may. My name is John.
exchanges.good_reply: Nice! Can you introduce yourself please? 😊
exchanges.wrong_reply: The question asks for your name. Example: 'My name is John.' Please try again.

exchanges.question: Could you introduce your friend to me?
exchanges.example_answer: Yes, I could. This is my friend, Mr. Smith.
exchanges.good_reply: Great! Now tell me about your friend. 👤
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try to introduce your friend. Example: 'Yes, I could. This is my friend [name].'

exchanges.question: What is her name?
exchanges.example_answer: Her name is Sarah.
exchanges.wrong_reply: The question asks for her name. Example: 'Her name is Sarah.'

exchanges.question: What is his name?
exchanges.example_answer: His name is John.
exchanges.good_reply: I am glad to meet you! 🎉
exchanges.wrong_reply: The question asks for his name. Example: 'His name is John.'

exchanges.question: How do you say 'Enchanté' in English?
expected_answers: it has been nice meeting you
it was a big pleasure to know you
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: 'Nice to meet you' or 'Me too.'

exchanges.question: What are your feelings about WEC-BEC?
exchanges.example_answer: For me, WEC-BEC is the best center. I am so happy to be here.
exchanges.good_reply: Are you happy to be here at WEC-BEC? 🏆
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me how you feel about WEC-BEC. Example: 'I am very happy to be here.'

exchanges.question: Are you happy to be here at WEC-BEC?
exchanges.example_answer: Of course, I am so happy to be here at WEC-BEC.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Answer the question. Example: 'Yes, I am very happy to be here.'

exchanges.question: What do you mean by that?
exchanges.example_answer: I mean that English is very important.
exchanges.good_reply: What does pawpaw mean in French? 🍈
exchanges.wrong_reply: Explain what you mean. Example: 'I mean [explanation].'

exchanges.question: What does pawpaw mean in French?
exchanges.good_reply: What is the meaning of 'host family' in French? 🏠
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. 'Pawpaw' means 'la papaye' in French.

exchanges.question: What is the meaning of 'host family' in French?
exchanges.good_reply: How do you say 'le riz' in English? 🍚
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. 'Host family' means 'famille d'accueil' in French.

exchanges.question: How do you say 'le riz' in English?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. 'Le riz' is 'rice' in English.

exchanges.question: What does WEC-BEC stand for?
expected_answers: world english club, basic english center
world english club basic english center
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. WEC-BEC stands for 'world english club, basic english center.'

exchanges.question: How do you say 'fat' in French?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. 'Fat' in French is 'gros.'

conversations.title: 📅 Lesson 10: There is / There was / There will be

exchanges.question: Can you tell me about the different uses of 'il y a', 'il y avait', and 'il y aura'?
exchanges.example_answer: In the present tense we use 'there is' and 'there are'. In the past tense we use 'there was' and 'there were'. In the future tense we use 'there will be'.
exchanges.good_reply: Give me an example with 'there is'! ✨
exchanges.wrong_reply: Explain the three tenses. Example: 'Present = there is/are, Past = there was/were, Future = there will be.'

conversations.title: 🔤 Lesson 11: Indefinite Article: A and An

exchanges.question: What's the difference between 'a' and 'an'?
expected_answers: a is used before consonant sounds and an before vowel sounds
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! Give me an example with 'a'. 📝
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: 'We use 'a' before consonants and 'an' before vowels.'

exchanges.question: What's the difference between 'at' and 'to'?
expected_answers: at is used when there is no movement
to is used when there is movement
at for static position to for direction
exchanges.good_reply: Correct! Give me an example with 'at'. 🏠
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Say: 'At' is for no movement, 'To' is for movement/direction.

exchanges.question: When do we use the possessive case?
exchanges.example_answer: We use the possessive case to express possession or ownership.
exchanges.good_reply: How do you say 'the house of Greg' using possessive case? 🏡
exchanges.wrong_reply: Explain when to use the possessive case. Example: 'To show possession or ownership.'

exchanges.question: How do you say 'the house of Greg' in possessive case?
exchanges.good_reply: What about plural? How do you say 'the house of the Gregs'? 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. 'Greg's house' is the correct possessive form.

exchanges.question: How do you say 'the house of the Gregs' in possessive case (plural)?
exchanges.wrong_reply: For plural, put apostrophe after 's': 'Gregs' house'.

exchanges.question: What time is it?
exchanges.example_answer: It's three o'clock.
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! Now you can tell time in English! 🕐
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell the current time. Example: 'It's [number] o'clock' or 'It's [minutes] past [hour].'

exchanges.question: How did you spend your night?
exchanges.example_answer: I spent a good night.
exchanges.good_reply: What was our last lesson about? 📚
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me about your night. Example: 'I spent a good night.'

exchanges.question: What is a decade?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. A decade is a period of ten years.

exchanges.question: What is a century?
expected_answers: a period of one hundred years
exchanges.good_reply: How many seasons are there in Europe? 🌍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. A century is a period of one hundred years.

exchanges.question: How many seasons are there in Europe?
exchanges.good_reply: How many seasons are there in central Africa? ☀️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. There are 4 seasons in Europe: Winter, Spring, Summer, Autumn.

exchanges.question: How many seasons are there in central Africa?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. There are 2 seasons in central Africa: dry season and rainy season.

exchanges.question: What is your full name?
exchanges.example_answer: My full name is John Smith.
exchanges.good_reply: How do you spell your name? 🔤
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your full name. Example: 'My full name is [first name + last name].'

exchanges.question: How do you spell your name?
exchanges.example_answer: I spell my name T-Y-C-O-O-N.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Spell your name letter by letter. Example: 'I spell my name J-O-H-N.'

exchanges.question: What is your email address?
exchanges.example_answer: My email address is john.smith@gmail.com.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Give me your email address. Example: 'My email address is [email].'

exchanges.question: What is your phone number?
exchanges.example_answer: My phone number is 06-488-69-23.
exchanges.good_reply: Perfect! Now I can contact you! 📱
exchanges.wrong_reply: Give me your phone number. Example: 'My phone number is [number].'

exchanges.question: Where are you coming from now?
exchanges.example_answer: I am coming from home.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me where you are coming from. Example: 'I am coming from [place].'

exchanges.question: Where were you?
exchanges.example_answer: I was at school.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me where you were. Example: 'I was at [place].'

exchanges.question: What is your nationality?
exchanges.example_answer: I am Congolese.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your nationality. Example: 'I am [nationality].'

exchanges.question: Where do you come from?
exchanges.example_answer: I come from Congo.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me where you are from. Example: 'I come from [country].'

exchanges.question: How old are you?
exchanges.example_answer: I am 20 years old.
exchanges.good_reply: What is the age gap between you and your brother? 👨‍👦
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your age. Example: 'I am [number] years old.'

exchanges.question: What is the age gap between you and your brother?
exchanges.example_answer: We have an age gap of three years.
exchanges.good_reply: Great! Now I know your age! 🎉
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me the difference in age. Example: 'We have an age gap of [number] years.'

conversations.title: 📅 Lesson 19: Date and Place of Birth

exchanges.question: When were you born?
exchanges.example_answer: I was born in 2000.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your birth year. Example: 'I was born in [year].'

exchanges.question: Where were you born?
exchanges.example_answer: I was born in Brazzaville.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your birth place. Example: 'I was born in [city].'

exchanges.question: When is your birthday?
exchanges.example_answer: My birthday is in August.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your birthday month. Example: 'My birthday is in [month].'

exchanges.question: Who brought you up?
exchanges.example_answer: My grandmother brought me up.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me who raised you. Example: 'My [grandmother/parents] brought me up.'

exchanges.question: What position are you in your family?
exchanges.example_answer: I am the eldest.
exchanges.good_reply: How many are you in your family? 👨‍👩‍👧‍👦
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your birth order. Example: 'I am the eldest' or 'I am the last child.'

exchanges.question: How many are you in your family?
exchanges.example_answer: We are seven in our family.
exchanges.good_reply: Thank you for sharing about your family! 💕
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me the size of your family. Example: 'We are [number] in our family.'

exchanges.question: What is your marital status?
exchanges.example_answer: I am single.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me if you are single, married, or divorced. Example: 'I am single.'

exchanges.question: Are you engaged to someone?
exchanges.example_answer: No, I am not engaged.
exchanges.good_reply: Have you got a boyfriend or girlfriend? 💕
exchanges.wrong_reply: Answer yes or no. Example: 'Yes, I am engaged to [name]' or 'No, I am not.'

exchanges.question: Have you got a boyfriend or girlfriend?
exchanges.example_answer: No, I haven't. I am free.
exchanges.good_reply: Are you faithful to your partner? 💖
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me about your relationship status. Example: 'Yes, I have a girlfriend' or 'No, I am single.'

conversations.title: 📆 Lesson 22: Days, Weeks, Months and Dates

exchanges.question: How many days are there in the week?
exchanges.good_reply: How many weeks are there in the month? 📅
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. There are seven days in the week.

exchanges.question: How many weeks are there in the month?
exchanges.good_reply: How many months are there in the year? 🗓️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. There are 4 weeks in a month.

exchanges.question: How many months are there in the year?
exchanges.good_reply: How many days are there in the year? 📆
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. There are 12 months in a year.

exchanges.question: How many days are there in the year?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. There are 365 days in a year.

exchanges.question: What is the date today?
exchanges.example_answer: Today is Monday, July 3rd.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me today's date. Example: 'Today is [day], [month] [date].'

exchanges.question: What was the date yesterday?
exchanges.example_answer: Yesterday was Tuesday, July 2nd.
exchanges.good_reply: What will be the date tomorrow? ⏩
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me yesterday's date. Example: 'Yesterday was [day], [month] [date].'

exchanges.question: What do you do during your free time?
exchanges.example_answer: During my free time I read books.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me what you like to do in your free time. Example: 'I like to read.'

exchanges.question: Whose bag is this?
exchanges.example_answer: It is mine.
exchanges.good_reply: Who is the owner of this house? 🏠
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me who the bag belongs to. Example: 'It is mine' or 'It belongs to me.'

exchanges.question: Who is the owner of this house?
exchanges.example_answer: I am the owner of this house.
exchanges.good_reply: To whom does this pen belong? ✒️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me who owns the house. Example: 'I am the owner.'

exchanges.question: To whom does this pen belong?
exchanges.example_answer: It belongs to me.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me who the pen belongs to. Example: 'It belongs to me.'

exchanges.question: What are your likes and dislikes?
exchanges.example_answer: I like fruits and I dislike vegetables.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me what you like and dislike. Example: 'I like [something] and dislike [something].'

exchanges.question: What is your favorite dish?
exchanges.example_answer: My favorite dish is meat.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your favorite food. Example: 'My favorite dish is [food].'

exchanges.question: What is your pet aversion?
exchanges.example_answer: I hate beer.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me something you hate. Example: 'I hate [something].'

exchanges.question: Where do you study English?
exchanges.example_answer: I study English at WEC-BEC.
exchanges.good_reply: How often do you study per week? 🕐
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me where you learn English. Example: 'I study at [center name].'

exchanges.question: How often do you study per week?
exchanges.example_answer: We study three times a week.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me how many times a week you study. Example: 'I study [number] times a week.'

exchanges.question: Which university do you attend?
exchanges.example_answer: I attend Marien Ngouabi University.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your university name. Example: 'I attend [university name].'

exchanges.question: What is your major?
exchanges.example_answer: My major is English.
exchanges.good_reply: Have you moved up to the next class? ⬆️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your field of study. Example: 'My major is [subject].'

exchanges.question: What is your level?
exchanges.example_answer: I am in the first level of secondary school.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your grade or level. Example: 'I am in [level].'

exchanges.question: What do you want to do in life?
exchanges.example_answer: I want to be a doctor.
exchanges.good_reply: What is your goal in life? 🎯
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your dream job. Example: 'I want to be a [profession].'

exchanges.question: What is your goal in life?
exchanges.example_answer: My goal is to travel abroad.
exchanges.good_reply: Why do you want to learn English? 💪
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me what you want to achieve. Example: 'My goal is to [action].'

exchanges.question: Why do you want to learn English?
exchanges.example_answer: I want to learn English because I want to travel abroad.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your reason for learning English. Example: 'I want to learn English because [reason].'

exchanges.question: What is your hobby?
exchanges.example_answer: My hobby is playing soccer.
exchanges.good_reply: Great! Hobbies are important for relaxation! 🎯
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me what you like to do for fun. Example: 'My hobby is [activity].'

exchanges.question: Can you speak English?
exchanges.example_answer: I am still a beginner, but I am learning.
exchanges.good_reply: What is the secret of learning? 🔑
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me about your English level. Example: 'I am a beginner' or 'I can speak a little.'

exchanges.question: What is the secret of learning?
exchanges.example_answer: You must read a lot and practice every day.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me how to learn well. Example: 'You must read a lot and practice.'

exchanges.question: Why is learning languages important?
exchanges.example_answer: If you know many languages, you will easily find a job.
exchanges.good_reply: Can you convey a message in English now? 📨
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me the benefits of knowing languages. Example: 'It helps you get a job.'

exchanges.question: Can you convey a message in English now?
exchanges.example_answer: Yes, I can try to speak English.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Answer yes or no. Example: 'Yes, I can communicate a little.'

conversations.title: 🔍 Lesson 30: To Look For and To Wait For

exchanges.question: What are you looking for?
exchanges.example_answer: I am looking for my wallet.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me what you are searching for. Example: 'I am looking for [object/person].'

exchanges.question: Who are you waiting for?
exchanges.example_answer: I am waiting for my friend.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me who you are expecting. Example: 'I am waiting for [person].'

exchanges.question: What does your father do?
exchanges.example_answer: My father is a doctor.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your father's job. Example: 'My father is a [profession].'

exchanges.question: What does your mother do?
exchanges.example_answer: My mother is a teacher.
exchanges.good_reply: What do you want to be? 💼
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your mother's job. Example: 'My mother is a [profession].'

exchanges.question: What do you want to be?
exchanges.example_answer: I want to be an engineer.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your dream job. Example: 'I want to be a [profession].'

exchanges.question: Do you pray?
exchanges.example_answer: Yes, I do. I am a Christian.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Answer yes or no. Example: 'Yes, I pray' or 'No, I don't.'

exchanges.question: Where do you pray?
exchanges.example_answer: I pray at the Catholic Church.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me where you worship. Example: 'I pray at [place].'

exchanges.question: What is your religion?
exchanges.example_answer: My religion is Christianity.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your religion. Example: 'I am Christian' or 'I am Muslim.'

exchanges.question: Have you ever been abroad?
exchanges.example_answer: No, I have never been abroad.
exchanges.good_reply: Would you like to travel overseas? 🌍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Answer yes or no. Example: 'Yes, I have been abroad' or 'No, I haven't.'

exchanges.question: Would you like to travel overseas?
exchanges.example_answer: Yes, I would love to travel abroad.
exchanges.good_reply: I hope you get to travel soon! ✈️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me if you want to travel. Example: 'Yes, I want to travel abroad.'

exchanges.question: What is wrong with you?
exchanges.example_answer: I am sick. I have a headache.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me what is bothering you. Example: 'I have a headache.'

exchanges.question: What are you suffering from?
exchanges.example_answer: I am suffering from a fever.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me your symptoms. Example: 'I have a fever' or 'My leg hurts.'

exchanges.question: What is the capital city of Congo?
exchanges.good_reply: What is the official language in Congo? 🗣️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. The capital of Congo is Brazzaville.

exchanges.question: What is the official language in Congo?
exchanges.good_reply: What are the national languages in Congo? 🌍
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. The official language of Congo is French.

exchanges.question: What are the national languages in Congo?
exchanges.good_reply: What are the colors of the Congolese flag? 🏁
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. The national languages are Lingala and Kituba.

exchanges.question: What are the colors of the Congolese flag?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. The colors of the Congolese flag are green, yellow and red.

exchanges.question: What is the Congolese currency?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. The currency is CFA franc.

exchanges.question: What are the advantages of reading?
exchanges.example_answer: Reading helps you discover new things and become knowledgeable.
exchanges.good_reply: What are the disadvantages of reading too much? 📚
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me the benefits of reading. Example: 'Reading helps you learn new things.'

exchanges.question: What are the disadvantages of reading too much?
exchanges.example_answer: If you read too much, you can become crazy or get eye problems.
exchanges.good_reply: Good analysis! Everything has pros and cons! ⚖️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me the negative effects of reading too much. Example: 'You can get tired or have eye problems.'

exchanges.question: Who discovered America?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Christopher Columbus discovered America in 1492.

exchanges.question: Who invented the telephone?
exchanges.good_reply: Who was the first person on the moon? 🌙
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone in 1876.

exchanges.question: Who was the first person on the moon?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. Neil Armstrong was the first person on the moon in 1969.

exchanges.question: Who created the airplane?
exchanges.wrong_reply: Try again. The Wright brothers created the airplane in 1903.

exchanges.question: How long have you been learning English?
exchanges.example_answer: I have been learning English for two years.
exchanges.good_reply: How long ago did you start learning English? 🗓️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me how long you have studied English. Example: 'I have been learning for [time].'

exchanges.question: How long ago did you start learning English?
exchanges.example_answer: I started learning English two years ago.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me when you started. Example: 'I started [number] years/months ago.'

exchanges.question: How much is this?
exchanges.example_answer: It is one thousand CFA.
exchanges.wrong_reply: Tell me the price. Example: 'It is [amount] CFA francs.'

exchanges.question: Do you have enough money?
exchanges.example_answer: No, I am short of money. I can't afford it.
exchanges.good_reply: I hope you can buy it another time! 🛍️
exchanges.wrong_reply: Answer yes or no. Example: 'Yes, I have enough money' or 'No, I don't.'
